hypothesis: If open-weight language models are first supervisedly fine-tuned on BCC/B2 composition triples and then trained with Direct Preference Optimization using Thermo-Calc–derived hierarchical rewards, then the DPO-tuned models will generate BCC/B2 alloy compositions with higher mean synthesis reward, greater compositional validity, improved coverage and novelty, and acceptable lattice mismatch (i.e., better overall design quality) than the SFT models and baseline methods, because physics-based preference feedback steers generations toward thermodynamically favorable and synthesis-relevant candidates.
